title: The Prevalence, Risk Factors, and Outcomes of Sepsis in Critically Ill Patients in China: A Multicenter Prospective Cohort Study

sample_size.source_quote: A total of 4,910 patients were enrolled in the study. Of these, 2,086 (42.5%) presented with sepsis or septic shock on admission to the ICU or within the first 48 h after admission to the ICU.

icu_mortality_percent.source_quote: The ICU mortality rates were 27.2% in patients with sepsis and 9.5% in those without sepsis

mortality_predictors.outcome_definition: ICU mortality in all patients with sepsis
mortality_predictors.confounders_adjusted: age; sex; APACHE II score; SOFA-initial score; comorbid conditions; use of MV, RRT and vasopressors; sites of infection; isolated organisms; use of antibiotics within 1 h; use of antibiotics pre-sepsis; and septic shock

mortality_predictors.predictor_variable: APACHE II score (per one point increase)
mortality_predictors.outcome_definition: ICU mortality in all patients with sepsis
mortality_predictors.confounders_adjusted: age; sex; APACHE II score; SOFA-initial score; comorbid conditions; use of MV, RRT and vasopressors; sites of infection; isolated organisms; use of antibiotics within 1 h; use of antibiotics pre-sepsis; and septic shock
mortality_predictors.source_quote: APACHE II, for per one point increase 1.16 (1.13–1.21) < 0.001
